description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, rectangular form and a dark woodgrain finish covering most of its exterior. The front features a large, convex glass screen bordered by a thick, light-colored metallic or plastic bezel, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. To the right of the screen, there is a vertical control panel with a large rotary dial and a ribbed speaker grille below it, both set into a contrasting panel. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the wood texture, while the back is not visible but is likely to have ventilation grilles and input/output ports typical of older televisions. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with sharp edges and a slightly protruding control section. This television would be a common sight in urban settings such as old apartments, garages, or curbside for disposal.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex screen, and a side control panel featuring a rotary dial and speaker grille.
description_view_0: The left side of the television, showing a smooth, uninterrupted woodgrain surface with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_1: The back-right angled view, highlighting the protruding control panel and the woodgrain finish, but the screen is not visible.
description_view_2: The right side of the television, similar to the left, with a smooth woodgrain surface and the edge of the control panel slightly visible.
description_view_3: The front view, displaying the large convex screen, thick light bezel, rotary dial, and ribbed speaker grille on the right.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, light gray bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and rounded corners, likely made of plastic or metal.
attribute_control_button_layout: Single large rotary dial and speaker grille on the right front panel; no other buttons visible.
